message:
fix(core): preserve iteration order in conflict detection and diff

Fix non-deterministic behavior in conflict detection and section diff
computation caused by iterating over HashMaps.

## Problem: HashMap Iteration Order

HashMaps in Rust have arbitrary iteration order. The previous code:

  let higher_sections: HashMap<&str, &SkillSection> =
      higher.sections.iter().map(|s| (s.id.as_str(), s)).collect();
  for (id, higher_section) in &higher_sections { ... }

This caused:
1. Non-deterministic conflict ordering in reports
2. Flaky tests that depend on conflict order
3. Inconsistent diff output between runs

## Fix: Iterate Source Collections Directly

### detect_section_conflicts (src/core/layering.rs)

Before: Iterate higher_sections HashMap
After: Iterate higher.sections Vec directly, lookup in lower_sections map

  for higher_section in &higher.sections {
      if let Some(lower_section) = lower_sections.get(higher_section.id.as_str()) {

### compute_section_diff (src/core/layering.rs)

Before: Iterate higher_blocks and lower_blocks HashMaps
After: Iterate higher.blocks and lower.blocks Vecs directly

  for block in &higher.blocks {
      if !lower_blocks_map.contains_key(block.id.as_str()) { ... }

This preserves the original section/block ordering from the skill
specification, making output deterministic and matching user expectations.

## Additional Change (src/core/packing.rs)

Minor: Added blank line for readability (no functional change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
